# verusidx-mcp — Full Reference

> 7 MCP servers giving AI agents direct local access to the Verus blockchain — 48 tools, zero cloud dependencies.

## Overview

verusidx-mcp is a suite of Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers for the Verus blockchain. MCP is Anthropic's open protocol for connecting AI models to external tools. Each server runs as a local stdio process, communicating with a local Verus daemon (verusd) via JSON-RPC over localhost. No API keys, no cloud services, no intermediaries between the agent and the chain.

The project is a pnpm monorepo with 8 packages: 7 MCP servers and 1 shared library.

## Architecture

```
AI Client (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.)
    |
    |  stdio (local process, no network)
    v
verusidx MCP servers (7 servers, 48 tools)
    |
    |  JSON-RPC over localhost
    v
verusd (one or more local Verus daemons)
```

- Each MCP server runs as a separate process via stdio transport
- @verusidx/shared provides: registry reader, RPC client with credential caching, error normalization (10 categories), audit logging, spending limit enforcement, read-only guard
- Chain registry (chains.json) is written atomically by chain-mcp, read by all other servers via stat()-based cache invalidation
- Credentials are read from daemon .conf files — never stored by MCP servers

## Prerequisites

- A running Verus daemon (verusd). Download from https://verus.io/wallet
- Node.js >= 18.0.0

## Installation

Add servers to your MCP client config. chain-mcp is the foundation — install it first:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "verusidx-chain": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@verusidx/chain-mcp"]
    },
    "verusidx-identity": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@verusidx/identity-mcp"]
    },
    "verusidx-send": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@verusidx/send-mcp"]
    },
    "verusidx-data": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@verusidx/data-mcp"]
    },
    "verusidx-address": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@verusidx/address-mcp"]
    },
    "verusidx-marketplace": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@verusidx/marketplace-mcp"]
    },
    "verusidx-definecurrency": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@verusidx/definecurrency-mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

After starting, call `refresh_chains` to discover local daemons. Every tool requires a `chain` parameter (e.g., "VRSC", "vrsctest") — there is no default chain.

## Complete Tool List

### @verusidx/chain-mcp (11 tools)

Foundation server. Install first.

| Tool | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| getinfo | read | Get blockchain and node info |
| getwalletinfo | read | Get wallet balances and state |
| help | read | Get daemon RPC documentation for any command |
| getblockcount | read | Get current block height (lightweight) |
| getcurrency | read | Get full currency definition and state (reserves, weights, prices, fees) |
| status | read | Check registry freshness and daemon reachability |
| refresh_chains | discovery | Re-scan filesystem and rewrite chain registry |
| verusd | system | Start a daemon instance |
| stop | write | Stop a running daemon |
| sendrawtransaction | write | Broadcast a signed raw transaction hex |
| signrawtransaction | write | Sign inputs of a raw transaction |

### @verusidx/identity-mcp (11 tools)

VerusID identity management.

| Tool | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| getidentity | read | Look up identity by name or i-address |
| getidentitycontent | read | Get identity content with optional VDXF key filter and height range |
| getidentityhistory | read | Full revision history of an identity |
| getvdxfid | read | Resolve VDXF URI to on-chain i-address |
| listidentities | read | List wallet's VerusIDs (spendable, signable, watch-only) |
| registernamecommitment | write | Step 1 of identity registration — reserve name |
| registeridentity | write | Step 2 of identity registration — register with commitment |
| updateidentity | write | Update identity fields |
| revokeidentity | write | Revoke an identity |
| recoveridentity | write | Recover a compromised identity |
| setidentitytimelock | write | Set or modify identity timelock |

### @verusidx/send-mcp (8 tools)

Currency operations.

| Tool | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| getcurrencybalance | read | Multi-currency balances for an address |
| getcurrencyconverters | read | Find fractional baskets for currency conversions |
| estimateconversion | read | Preview conversion output with fees and slippage |
| listcurrencies | read | List or search currencies with filters |
| z_getoperationstatus | read | Check status of async operations |
| gettransaction | read | Get transaction details by txid |
| listtransactions | read | List recent wallet transactions with pagination |
| sendcurrency | write | Send, convert, or cross-chain transfer currency (async, returns opid) |

### @verusidx/data-mcp (7 tools)

On-chain data retrieval, decryption, signing, and verification.

| Tool | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| z_listreceivedbyaddress | read | List transactions/data received at a z-address |
| decryptdata | read | Decrypt on-chain data with viewing key or wallet keys |
| z_exportviewingkey | read | Export viewing key for read-only decryption access |
| z_viewtransaction | read | View detailed shielded transaction information |
| signdata | read | Sign data with a VerusID or address (available in read-only mode) |
| verifysignature | read | Verify a signature (available in read-only mode) |
| z_importviewingkey | write | Import a viewing key for decryption access |

### @verusidx/marketplace-mcp (5 tools)

On-chain atomic swap marketplace.

| Tool | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| getoffers | read | Browse offers for a currency or identity |
| listopenoffers | read | List wallet's open offers |
| makeoffer | write | Create an on-chain atomic swap offer |
| takeoffer | write | Accept an existing offer (atomic exchange) |
| closeoffers | write | Close/cancel offers and reclaim locked funds |

### @verusidx/address-mcp (5 tools)

Address management.

| Tool | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| validateaddress | read | Validate address and return info |
| getaddressesbyaccount | read | List transparent addresses for an account |
| z_listaddresses | read | List shielded addresses in wallet |
| getnewaddress | write | Generate a new transparent R-address |
| z_getnewaddress | write | Generate a new shielded Sapling zs-address |

### @verusidx/definecurrency-mcp (1 tool)

Currency definition.

| Tool | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| definecurrency | write | Define a new currency — returns signed tx hex to broadcast via sendrawtransaction |

## Configuration

### Environment Variables (all servers)

| Variable | Description | Default |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| VERUSIDX_READ_ONLY | Set to "true" to disable write tools entirely | false |
| VERUSIDX_AUDIT_LOG | Set to "false" to disable audit logging | true |
| VERUSIDX_AUDIT_DIR | Custom audit log directory | OS config dir |
| VERUSIDX_SPENDING_LIMITS_PATH | Custom path to spending-limits.json | OS config dir |

### Environment Variables (chain-mcp only)

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| VERUSIDX_DATA_DIR | Non-standard Verus data directory |
| VERUSIDX_EXTRA_CHAINS | Remote daemons (format: name:host:port:user:pass) |
| VERUSIDX_BIN_PATH | Directory containing verusd binary |

### Read-Only Mode

Set `VERUSIDX_READ_ONLY=true` per-server via MCP client config env. Write tools are not registered — they don't appear in the tool list. Set independently per server for fine-grained control (e.g., identity-mcp read-write, send-mcp read-only).

Exceptions: signdata and verifysignature (data-mcp) remain available in read-only mode — signing doesn't spend funds or change state.

### Spending Limits

Servers that send funds (send-mcp, marketplace-mcp) enforce per-currency limits via spending-limits.json. Default created on first run: `{ "VRSC": 10 }`. Limits are checked before the RPC call — blocked transactions never reach the daemon.

File location:
- macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/verusidx-mcp/spending-limits.json
- Linux: ~/.config/verusidx-mcp/spending-limits.json
- Windows: %APPDATA%\verusidx-mcp\spending-limits.json

Format: `{ "VRSC": 100, "Bridge.vETH": 0.5 }`. Currency names are case-insensitive. Multi-output sends sum all outputs per currency.

### Audit Logging

All write operations are logged to date-stamped append-only JSONL files (0600 permissions). Each entry records: timestamp, server, tool, chain, full parameters, result, success flag.

Log location:
- macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/verusidx-mcp/audit/
- Linux: ~/.config/verusidx-mcp/audit/
- Windows: %APPDATA%\verusidx-mcp\audit\

## About Verus

Verus is an open-source, fair-launch blockchain (2018, no ICO, no premine, no VC funding). Hybrid PoW/PoS consensus with CPU-mineable VerusHash 2.2. No smart contracts — identity, data storage, DeFi conversions, atomic swaps, privacy (Sapling zk-proofs), and cross-chain bridges (PBaaS) are implemented as consensus-level protocol features.

Key concepts:
- VerusID: protocol-level identity with addresses, signing keys, revocation/recovery authorities, on-chain data storage (contentmultimap), and optional shielded private address
- VDXF: Verus Data Exchange Format — namespaced key system for on-chain data (e.g., vrsc::identity.profile.name)
- PBaaS: Public Blockchains as a Service — launch independent interoperable chains with protocol-level bridges
- Fractional baskets: multi-reserve currencies with automatic AMM-style conversions, MEV-resistant
- Sapling: zero-knowledge proof system for shielded (private) transactions

- Verus: https://verus.io
- Verus docs: https://docs.verus.io
